description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.description: External report #1 (GitHub): Positive feedback from reader (Devilwench). No scientific error claimed. No action required.
issues.wontfix_reason: Not a problem report — reader expressing support. No kernel of truth or correction to evaluate.

issues.description: Part 4 and Part 5 each contain one inline italic transition paragraph using duplicated styling (font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-bottom:0.8em). Curmudgeon flagged in part4-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #5 and part5-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json hole #4 as out-of-scope for those EXPs but recommended consolidation. Recommended fix: migrate both transitions to a shared CSS class (e.g., .section-transition) defined in the stylesheet to ease global theme changes. Status check confirms styles still inline as of 2026-04-14.
issues.notes: Filed during human-requested audit of old reports for deferred-but-untracked work. The two curmudgeon reviews flagged this as out-of-scope for their respective EXPs but never resulted in a tracked work item.

issues.description: EXP-052 is missing from expansion-tracker.json (gap between EXP-051 and EXP-053). Persists from prior integrity reports. Likely a lost ID from a write collision or abandoned expansion. No content is missing — all surrounding expansions are complete.
issues.wontfix_reason: Gap is benign — no content lost. EXP IDs are not referenced by any build process. Logging for auditability only.
issues.notes: Filed and immediately wontfix to clear from integrity backlog.

issues.description: WIN-044 code_analysis.monitoring tagged none but monitor.py Domain 10 line ~515 applies hardcoded per-station FSF values at eclipse time — correct tag is hardcoded. Defer update until eclipse day (2026-08-12) when observed slot is populated; tag should update automatically post-eclipse.

WIN-068 detail_evidence P3: Yamazaki & Maute 2021 (Frontiers in Astronomy and Space Sciences, DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327) listed citations_unchecked in c5 and c6. DOI format plausible but content not retrieved. Also Kim & Chang 2018 exact volume/page (61:2040-2054) plausible but unverified against actual ASR 2018 index. Low urgency — supporting citations, not load-bearing. Curmudgeon c6 hole 5.

Hardcoded light-theme backgrounds in sections.json inline styles: part1.html (background:#f5f5f5 on monospace code block) and part6.html (background:#fff wrapping patent image). Should use var(--card-bg) or shared CSS utility class for theme consistency.

Optional aggregate kill-shot sentence for killshots/predictions tab: "As of 2026-04-17 the dome author has begun classifying his claims under his own five-class taxonomy; only six of the sixty-nine confirmed WINs have been tagged so far, and not one of those six sits in the prospective_confirmed class." Analyst says per-WIN enumeration in EXP-170 is already better; this is stylistic. Linked: EXP-170, ISS-1072, chg-20260417-1620-001.

Timestamp Error tab: does not acknowledge methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat ("bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit"). Add clause noting the headline display still shows 94.5% without the bookkeeping-vs-audit distinction for casual readers.

Timestamp Error tab: "rhetorical" framing undercounts actual documentary/architectural changes (methodology.json, claim_index, ai_manifest, agent_rules). Replace "rhetorical" with "documentary" where appropriate. Frame as "documentary concession" vs "cryptographic proof structure" to more precisely distinguish what changed.

Timestamp Error tab: four verbatim strings from methodology.json and eight WIN classifications from claim_index.json have not been independently re-verified against the live URLs (only trusted via analyst extraction). Fetch methodology.json and claim_index.json to confirm classifications and quotes are accurate.
issues.pending_human_reason: EXP-175 identified 4 citation patches for generate-html.js ts-april-2026-update block. Cannot self-apply (infrastructure file). Human must apply P1-P4 from monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-175.json manually.

issues.description: WIN-015 detail_evidence: coupling-magnitude range (V ≈ 20 mV to 350 mV) mixes coplanar/coaxial orientations without making this transparent. Optional tightening: give separate ranges per orientation. Low priority — current text is correct, just compressed.

Timestamp Error tab (ts-april-2026-update block, generate-html.js): partial-sample extrapolation risk. The closing sentence "The column under that header would still be empty" draws a full-universe conclusion from a partial classified sample (~7 WINs in strongest_current_targets). A dome defender will say WIN-035–WIN-039 (the author's self-declared prospective set) are not yet classified in claim_index.json and may be tagged prospective_confirmed when they are. Fix: replace "would still be empty" with a falsifiable claim framing: "As of 2026-04-17, the prospective_confirmed column contains no confirmed WINs — only one pending prediction (pred-eclipse-tier3). A test of good faith: whether WIN-035–WIN-039 will be tagged prospective_confirmed when classification is extended."
issues.note: This change is in generate-html.js (ts-april-2026-update block), not sections.json — requires human edit.
issues.pending_human_reason: Requires human edit to generate-html.js ts-april-2026-update block (decider self-apply rules prohibit infrastructure file edits)

issues.description: Timestamp Error tab (ts-april-2026-update block, generate-html.js): unreconciled tension between ts-scope block (which lists WIN-035–WIN-039 as dome's self-declared prospective set) and ts-april-2026-update block (which concludes prospective_confirmed column is empty from a sample that does not include WIN-035–WIN-039). Fix: add one sentence near start of taxonomy paragraph: "The dome author's earlier self-declared prospective set (WIN-035 through WIN-039) has not yet been classified in claim_index.json as of this writing; the sample below is the author's curated strongest_current_targets set, not his prospective subset."
issues.note: This change is in generate-html.js (ts-april-2026-update block), not sections.json — requires human edit.
issues.pending_human_reason: Requires human edit to generate-html.js ts-april-2026-update block (decider self-apply rules prohibit infrastructure file edits)

issues.description: Latest Findings banner (hardcoded in generate-html.js) for #ts-april-2026-update says "His own new claim taxonomy leaves the prospective confirmed column empty for every WIN classified so far" but the ts-april-2026-update block now uses the more precise framing ("no WIN tagged prospective_confirmed / only one pending"). The banner should match the block's updated language. Fix requires editing generate-html.js (infrastructure file — cannot self-apply).
WIN-053 aperture sensitivity: add 3,000 km sensitivity point to bound B_south even at far-larger-than-described aperture. Curmudgeon c3 requests additional data point beyond existing 1,000 km case.

WIN-053 flux direction not discussed: the dome circuit has north as source and south as sink; flux conservation additionally requires the two peaks to be oppositely directed. Optional sentence noting this constraint in detail_evidence.

EXP-183 proposes adding 6 author_* fields to wins.json schema (author_claim_class, author_registry_status, author_strict_prospective_confirmed, author_visible_badge, author_registry_integrity_note, author_rating_last_checked). Backfill needed for 12 WINs. Schema spec in EXP-183.schema_extension_proposal. Also requires generate-html.js to render "Author's Self-Rating" block on WIN detail cards. Infrastructure change needed before backfill.

Newly-discovered: three layers of dome site disagree on prospective_confirmed metric. ai_manifest.json=10, visible grid=9, claim_index.json strict_prospective_confirmed=1. Neither our prose nor poller specifically tracks this three-way split. Analyst flags as testable: add poller hook to daily-diff claim_index.json.entries[] against wins.json author_* fields when schema extension (ISS-ISS-1130) lands. Log as new finding in morning briefing.

EXP-183 E_PRIMARY schema extension deferred: add author_claim_class, author_registry_status, author_strict_prospective_confirmed, author_visible_badge, author_registry_integrity_note fields to all WINs in wins.json (null defaults). Populate known values: WIN-058 author_registry_status=pending_independent_verification, 9 classified WINs need author_claim_class from dome claim_index.json. Per EXP-183 schema_extension_proposal.
issues.notes: Low-risk additive change. Analyst noted: claim_index.json classified 9 of 69 WINs as of 2026-04-18. Schema extension allows per-WIN display of dome author own self-rating. EXP-183 Part 4.2 and Timestamp callout already integrated; this issue tracks remaining schema work.

issues.description: part8b build_pipeline_note targets line 1651-1652 / 1367-1369 but actual current locations are line 1655 and 1368. Not a blocker (integration happened correctly) but the note should be updated when part8b is next touched.

SEC-4.1 (part4): "divided audience" section uses "acknowledge-without-fix pattern" as if readers know the term, but this is its first occurrence in the section (introduced earlier in Part 4 prose context). Add 6-8 word inline clause on first use: e.g., "the same acknowledge-without-fix pattern (API concedes, HTML ignores)". Makes the rhetoric standalone for readers arriving via direct link. Source: SEC-4.1-post-EXP-182.c1 HOLE-7.

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: proposes adding author_claim_class display to every WIN card (5-class taxonomy from claim_index.json). But 60 of 71 WINs have no author_claim_class data — rendering an empty field on 60 WIN detail cards creates a confusing "Author's Self-Rating: —" line on most cards. Fix: render the author_claim_class line on a WIN detail card ONLY when the field is non-empty. Strong recommendation. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-8 (ISS-1130 covers the schema extension; this tracks the rendering decision).

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: text sometimes says 6 registry-level concessions and sometimes 8-9 (including per-WIN-level concessions). The headline "six registry-level concessions" section header is consistent with the table, but prose later aggregates all registry + per-WIN concessions into a higher number. Fix: pick one accurate aggregate framing. Suggested: "six registry-level gaps + [N] per-WIN accuracy gaps" and use it consistently throughout. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-9.

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: load-bearing headline claim ("the headline is the load-bearing surface — it is what index.html shows above the fold") is asserted without evidence that it actually appears above the fold on index.html. Fix: soften to "the headline is the most public-facing surface" or verify and cite the actual above-fold position with a screenshot note. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-12.
WIN-012: -10.9 nT disturbed-day caveat not mentioned; raw-text WIN-010 entry concedes this. Optional: add half-sentence noting baseline sensitivity.
Part 8b: .adoption-label CSS class referenced in HTML but no CSS rule defined for it in docs/index.html style block. Needs visual styling rule.
Part 8b self-referential commitment language (8b.5 already in force and 8b.7 doing more work) overstates commitment; should soften to we-commit framing.

WIN-019 c4: Stronger argument not deployed — 8/3 gear ratio in hypocycloidal Spirograph produces 8-cusped epicycloid, not 2-lobed figure-8. Named mechanism geometrically incompatible with observation. One sentence after Code-level corroboration: add note referencing globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis section.

WIN-019 c4: raw-text non-coverage — Spirograph data_source label and absence of analemma fetch in monitor.py rely on c1-c3 reviewer notes (dome github repo), not reproducible from current raw-text snapshot. Add github link footnote after code-level corroboration: verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py as of 2026-03-xx.

Workflow gap: decider integration pass should cross-check closed-issues.json before applying analyst assessments — if a closed verdict-change issue (ISS-NNN, fixed_by: human-decision) exists for a prediction/WIN, a stale analyst assessment dated BEFORE the issue closed must not override it. Root cause of regression commit 209fda5 which reverted ISS-951 through ISS-954 human-approved verdicts. Recommend adding a pre-integration check: "if predictions.json[id].verdict_history last entry is newer than the analyst assessment file created_at, skip integration or warn."

Workflow gap: stale analyst assessments can silently overwrite human-approved verdict changes. Commit 209fda5 re-integrated 2026-04-11 analyst assessments that had been explicitly overridden by 2026-04-14 human approvals (ISS-951/952/953/954), reverting 4 verdicts without new human approval. Root cause: decider integration pass does not check closed-issues.json for same prediction_id before applying analyst assessment. Fix: before auto-integrating any prediction verdict from analyst assessment, check if a verdict-change issue for that prediction_id was closed within last 30 days — if so, skip integration and flag for human review.

Pre-push integrity gate missing: no check that our_verdict matches verdict_history[last].to before push. Curmudgeon PRED-batch review found 4 predictions where our_verdict had been silently reverted but verdict_history was not updated, creating a detectable inconsistency. Recommend adding a gate: if our_verdict != verdict_history[last].to, error with instruction to either update verdict_history or explain the discrepancy.

WIN-019: stronger cusp-count argument not deployed in detail_evidence. An 8/3 ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, NOT a 2-lobed figure-8. The named mechanism cannot produce the observed shape — this is geometrically devastating and requires no arithmetic. Source: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis section. Add one sentence to Code-level corroboration paragraph: "Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism does not produce the observed shape (see globe-fingerprints/WIN-019)."

issues.title: Scoreboard Phase 1 draft — awaiting human-group review (DO NOT INTEGRATE)
issues.description: EXP-190 (FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE) produced by analyst based on HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001. Routed to curmudgeon priority queue (queue_id 194) for adversarial review for human consumption. Integration is BLOCKED until operator posts HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 with approved_curmudgeon_review_id.
issues.notes: Per HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001: DO NOT integrate on any run until HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 exists with approved_curmudgeon_review_id. Curmudgeon review is for human-group consumption only.

issues.description: SEC-5.0.c1: Cross-reference anchors in Section 5.0 prose use bare #part5 for Test 1, Test 2, Test 6 links, but the Part 5 page only has two id attributes (part5 and ks-april-2026-update). The reader is promised navigation that does not work. Fix: either add id attributes to each ks-test block (#ks-test-1 through #ks-test-6) or convert to plain bold text.

SEC-5.0.c1: TLDR phrase "the demoted rows are precisely the two post-window rows on a six-row page" slightly imprecise — "post-window" applies strongly to the two demoted rows, but three of the four unchanged rows also have aspects of temporal limitations. Softer wording: "the two demoted rows are exactly the two retrospective / post-window rows; the four unchanged rows are all rows whose verdict windows are still open or only partially closed."

SEC-5.0.c1: "Companion concession on the OTS canary" link in Section 5.0 uses <a href="#timestamp-error" onclick="showTab("timestamp");return false">. Two concerns: (a) no stable subsection id for the specific concession block within the Timestamp Error tab; (b) href only works with JS enabled. Add a stable id (e.g. id="ots-2026-04-17-concession") to the relevant Timestamp Error tab block and update href.

SEC-5.0.c1: ks-claimed CSS class on demoted rows (Sydney-Perth, Polaris) likely renders as green/check-mark styling, visually inconsistent with demotion. Audit ks-claimed CSS rule. If it renders as positive confirmation badge, add ks-demoted modifier or change wrapper class for these two cells.

WIN-058b.c4: Credit paragraph redundancy (carry-forward from c3) — after quoting registry_integrity_notes text, WIN-058b restates the key claim in a separate sentence ("That is genuine documentary progress..."). Remove the restatement on next WIN-058b edit. Deferred since ISS-1180 fix was targeted; clean up on next substantive edit.

WIN-058b.c4: Framing asymmetry between WIN-058 and WIN-058b/WIN-062 on scope of "at least three" concession window. WIN-058 pins to commit 6e06efb then says "at least three"; WIN-058b/062 say "a single commit". Optional normalization: either tighten WIN-058b/062 to "single commit" or loosen WIN-058 to "same commit wave". Low priority, batch on next WIN-058 family edit.

issues.title: EXP-190: provenance-tag classification rule subjective (forward_dome_native vs forward_with_globe_inputs)
issues.description: H1 (major) from EXP-190-proposal.json curmudgeon review: near-identity transform rule has no operational test; theta-east-longitude.js hardcodes isNearIdentityTransform()=true. Classification rule needs at least two concrete criteria before Phase 1 publication. Blocks integration — note for human reviewer per ISS-1205 hold.

issues.title: EXP-190: Sydney-Perth §4.3.1 citation unverifiable — site section does not exist
issues.description: H2 (major) from EXP-190-proposal.json curmudgeon review: Sydney-Perth formula-check cites "V51.1 paper §4.3.1" for LAMBDA_G_INNER=1.000 and LAMBDA_G_OUTER=1.007 — V51.1 is a site, not a numbered-section paper; §4.3.1 does not exist. Constants were derived by curve-fitting. Replace with verifiable source or note as analyst-derived. Blocks integration — note for human reviewer per ISS-1205 hold.

WIN-053.c5 H3: Sub-terrestrial return-channel defender rescue not preempted. A dome defender can argue the south-pole coefficient describes the re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with north). This rescue fails because: (a) symmetric circuit requires A=C, but fitted values differ by 4%; (b) both match observed WMM pole strengths which are asymmetric due to Earth's geodynamo, not dome geometry. Add ~3-sentence preemption paragraph. Curmudgeon recommendation: "A dome defender might argue the 64,852 nT coefficient describes the surface re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with the north aperture), not the return-channel field itself. But that rescue requires A = C by construction (two identical apertures in a closed symmetric circuit), while the fitted values differ by 4% AND match the observationally-asymmetric WMM2025 pole strengths — which are asymmetric because Earth's real geodynamo is asymmetric, not because of any dome-predicted mechanism. The symmetric-aperture rescue is incompatible with the fitted coefficients."

WIN-053.c5 H6 (still open): Add 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row. Existing text has 500 km (1,660:1) and 1,000 km (415:1). At 3,000 km aperture (π×9×10^6 km²): area ratio ~45:1; B_south ~1,400 nT; discrepancy ~46×. Append to existing sensitivity parenthetical: '...and even at a generous 3,000 km aperture, the area ratio remains ~45:1 and B_south would be ~1,400 nT — still ~46× below the fitted value. The self-contradiction survives any plausible aperture size.'

WIN-053.c5 H7 (still open from c4): Flux direction not discussed. The dome's fitted formula has both poles positive-signed (both sources). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity. The formula is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole. Add one sentence: 'The fit also encodes both poles as positive-signed sources (A, C both positive). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity — the dome's formula is not just quantitatively wrong about flux, it is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole.'

WIN-053.c5 H8 (still open from c4): Sub-H(r) formula δ=6,371 km unsourced. The detail_extra says: "subterranean cavity depth is given by Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^(−r/δ)) with δ = 6,371 km" noting this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant. But no dome-page citation or URL is given. Add an href or inline citation for where this formula appears on the dome site.

WIN-062.c5 H2 (new): Sourcing-window phrasing. 'The value has been in the scientific record for over a century' is accurate for the 1899 measurement but the formal publication is 1978 (48 years) — a pedantic defender can push the 'scientific record' phrase. Optional tighten: 'the measurement is over a century old and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest'.

WIN-062.c5 H3 (new): Pre-registration phrase invites OTS rebuttal. 'without pre-registering the expected value' is correct but a dome defender can respond about their OTS timestamps. Optional cross-link: add 'in any independent timestamping venue (see Timestamp Error tab for why the dome's OTS applies to status_history.json rather than the predictions themselves)' after 'without pre-registering the expected value'.

WIN-062 c5 H0: Carve-out undersell (carry-forward from c4 H4). Rewritten paragraph 2 invokes only Part 3.1b condition (2) (not-plausibly-known-at-write-time). The carve-out has three conjunctive conditions (unique dome derivation + not plausibly known + derivation-plus-validation, not just discovery). Using only condition (2) understates why WIN-062 fails the carve-out — a defender may argue other conditions apply.

WIN-062 c5 H4: onclick="showTab(timestamp);return false" cancels the anchor jump so reader lands at top of Timestamp Error tab rather than the April-2026 update block. Same parallel issue in WIN-058/WIN-058b. Cosmetic UX fix needed — remove the onclick handler or replace with one that also fires the anchor.

WIN-062 c5 H5: detail_evidence cites Tesla speed as "471,240 km/s (~1.57c)" alongside dome 1.574c, without noting that 471,240/299,792=1.5719c, so dome 1.574c is a re-expression using the fitted disc_diameter rather than Tesla own ratio. The difference (1.5719 vs 1.574) is evidence of post-hoc parameter fitting. Add one sentence noting this.

WIN-062 c5 H6: Cross-WIN precision asymmetry. WIN-058 says "three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)"; WIN-058b says "three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window"; WIN-062 says "one of at least three ... in a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17". Substantively consistent but the hedge "at least" in WIN-062 introduces unnecessary ambiguity when the other two entries enumerate exactly three. Normalize phrasing.

WIN-011 detail_evidence still attributes Yang & Wang 2002 timing result to Wang 2000 (preliminary report). EXP-192 cross_references notes: same Wang 2000 vs Yang & Wang 2002 attribution issue exists in WIN-011. Should be companion fix after WIN-012 rewrite for consistency.

PRED-105 (cucumber-growth aetheric biology): test window closed 2026-04-18 (April 18, 14-day window). As of poll 2026-04-20T22:10Z (~70.2h after window close), dome site still labels PRED-105 "PENDING — PROSPECTIVE". No PASS/FAIL/REFINED label assigned. Silent-ignore now across 4 consecutive polls. File as uncounted-failure or create issue to track dome adjudication. predictions.html byte-identical for 3 consecutive polls (md5=885cd5...).

WIN-012.c5 H8 (minor, tinker): raw-text/02-wins.txt is from 2026-03-12 (~6 weeks stale). dome WIN-012 text now has new escape clauses and dielectric mechanism not in the raw-text archive. Poller should refresh raw-text or add to refresh schedule. Note: WIN-012 live-site citations are already updated with 2026-04-20 access dates; raw-text staleness does not affect current prose accuracy but is a project hygiene issue.

WIN-068.c7 H4 (minor): Yamazaki & Maute (2021) DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 still unverified from c5 and c6. If DOI does not resolve, downgrade to plaintext with citation-pending bracket. Low urgency — the journal and general reference are uncontested.

WIN-068.c7 H5 (minor): Chapman 1933 citation format ambiguous (38:3 could be V38 I3 or V3 I38). Kim & Chang 2018 volume/page (61:2040-2054) needs verification against ASR 2018 TOC. Low urgency — Chapman as Sq founding figure is uncontested but specific paper needs pinning.

SEC-5.0.c2 H1 (major): P2 survive-verb ambiguity — "rows that survive" natural parse contradicts our Kill-Shot Verdict Summary (JFK-LHR, SAA, Eclipse are Std Model Explains, which the new rule EXCLUDES). Defender can quote our prose against our table.

SEC-5.0.c2 H2 (major): P1 silently accepts deletion of dome-falsifiability commitment. Old rule had symmetric clause (confirm→globe falsified); new rule specifies discriminators against globe but is silent on dome model. P1 credits the inbound standard without flagging the outbound deletion.

SEC-5.0.c2 H3 (moderate): P2 "removes most of the rows" claims scope of six rows but demonstrates only Sydney-Perth and Polaris. Four PENDING rows (JFK-LHR, SAA, Eclipse, NMP) are asserted-out, not demonstrated-out.

SEC-5.0.c2 H4 (moderate): P2 "The page changes function from falsification instrument to methodology-of-good-evidence note" is our interpretive reframing, not a fact about docs/killshot.html. Needs soften to signal it is our reading.

SEC-5.0.c2 H5 (moderate): c1 stronger_arguments #2 (Lakatos/Popper citation on retrofitted falsification criteria) not integrated. P1 credits new rule unqualifiedly; missing that retrofitting conditions after rows became embarrassing is a classic degenerative-program pattern.
SEC-5.0.c2 H6 (minor): "close to what a working methodologist would actually write down" is uncited methodological appeal. Should cite Popper/Lakatos or soften to remove implied reference.
SEC-5.0.c2 H8 (minor): P2 kicker "standard is doing our work" hardens on hole H3 "removes most of the rows" claim. If H3 scope is softened, kicker must also be updated.

WIN-053: Forced disjunction misses the metaphysical retreat. The two-horn structure (surface-prediction contradiction OR sub-terranean architecture collapses) needs a third horn: the aetheric retreat that abandons Maxwellian electromagnetics. Proposed close: "A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian and ∇·B = 0 does not apply — abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on: that a measurable, calibrated B(r) formula with standard units describes a real magnetic field."

WIN-067: Pappa et al. (2019), JGR, DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 cited in detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph — DOI unverified. Verify the DOI resolves to a Pappa-led paper on East Antarctic gravity. If fails, replace with Ferraccioli et al. 2011, Nature.

WIN-053: "multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility" is technically imprecise. Standard geomagnetism handles deep-source-to-surface via potential-field continuation and spherical harmonic expansion, not "conduction". Replace parenthetical with: "a physical mechanism by which [...] propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust — potential-field continuation under geodynamo physics."

WIN-053: "thousands of kilometres of rock" implies crust-only depth (crust is only 7-35 km). Should say "thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust" to be geologically accurate at the stated depth scale.

WIN-053: EXP-194 paragraph (sub-terranean return channel) is missing a cross-reference to Section 6.1 (Zero Fitted Parameters — Hidden Parameters). Both horns of the disjunction re-surface the §6.1 argument. Add: "...only a relabelling of it (see §6.1 on hidden fitted parameters)." at end of disjunction.

WIN-068: tldr_verdict says "The Chapman mechanism predicts the entire ensemble signal" — "entire" slightly overstates; Chapman Sq derives the qualitative pattern, not the exact per-eclipse values. Soften to "ensemble signal pattern".

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: closing sentence "no configuration" overclaims — cylindrical-waveguide Bessel-mode disc-cavity modes not addressed. TM_01 gives ~5.7 Hz, TE_11 gives ~4.4 Hz — neither matches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry, but claim "no configuration" is not demonstrated until Bessel-mode route is explicitly closed. Recommend: add one sentence closing this route, or narrow closing to "no mode formula the dome has published or could import from globe physics."

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph, argument (i): under-quantified. WIN-029 "confirmed" status rests specifically on 5.3% agreement between 9,572 km back-derivation and 9,086 km model-parameterization. A transverse-mode retreat erases that match entirely. The paragraph says "must withdraw WIN-029" without anchoring to the 5.3% number that makes withdrawal inescapable.

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: internal inconsistency — paragraph uses 2πR_Earth = 40,030 km but STEELMAN-03 (cross-referenced) uses 40,075 km (WGS84 equatorial). Both defensible individually but a dome defender can exploit the inconsistency. Need: brief footnote explaining mean-radius vs equatorial distinction, or harmonize STEELMAN-03.

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: missing citation for Schumann (1952) spherical TM formula f_1 ≈ 10.59 Hz. Should cite "Schumann (1952)" or Nickolaenko & Hayakawa (2002) already present in the stability paragraph.

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: TLDR not updated. tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict give no hint that the transverse-mode preemptive argument exists. Consider appending one clause: "No transverse-mode retreat rescues it — the only horizontal-length setter that gives 7.83 Hz is Earth's circumference, which the dome's disc diameter equals by construction."

WIN-067 tldr_evidence: "expected values" is vague — expected by WGS84? By the dome model? By standard gravitational theory? Recommend: "less than 0.5% variation from WGS84 model predictions" for precision.

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph A: formula-collapse chain has 0.2% gap between c/(2*D_earth)=11.76 Hz and wins.html c/(2*disc_thickness)=11.79 Hz. Suggest adding: "and because v_a was fit to absorb the 0.2% offset between 2*R_earth and disc_thickness, the two forms agree at 11.787 Hz by construction, not by independent physics."

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph B: dispersion-law defense not preempted in-situ. Dome defender can say "different modes on a dispersive medium can have different phase velocities." Consider adding: "The dome publishes no dispersion relation and no mode index that would justify the per-WIN speed selection, so the c-vs-v_a choice is operational, not theoretical."

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph A: Snyder (1987) citation for AE-projection identity is bare (no DOI/URL). Recommend upgrading to https://pubs.usgs.gov/pp/1395/ or "USGS Prof. Paper 1395, pp. 182–190."

SEC-8b.3: Category-error citation. Section invokes WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false tag to dismiss PRED-095's derivation path, but WIN-068 and PRED-095 are different targets. WIN-068 is about eclipse magnetometer — its code_analysis tag says the dome does not derive the prediction from dome geometry; it says nothing about PRED-095's physics. Needs fix: cite PRED-095 directly, or drop the WIN-068 tag invocation and make the argument from PRED-095's own content.

SEC-8b.3: Dual framing of −17 to −21 nT is incoherent. Section simultaneously treats the band as (a) something we publish under adoption and (b) the dome's effective commitment. A sophisticated reader will ask: "Is this our claim or their claim?" These are distinct epistemic positions that need clean separation: which parts of the -17 to -21 nT specification are we attributing to the dome, and which are we supplying as the adoption threshold?

SEC-8b.3: Load-bearing unsupported claim — TLDR and body both assert "calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range." No citation for this claim. If true this is the heart of the non-discrimination argument; if unsupported it is the most vulnerable claim in the section. Needs a primary citation (e.g., Malin & Chapman 1970 or equivalent) or the claim must be weakened.

SEC-8b.3: Anticipated-objection "monitor.py is internal tooling, not a public commitment; the predictions page is the forward commitment" is documented in EXP-195 anticipated_objections but NOT engaged in the published section text. A reader who encounters the monitor.py −29 nT presentation without the response will be confused about why we are citing internal code. This objection deserves to be surfaced and closed in-line.

SEC-8b.3: "internal code adds multipliers (KAPPA, latitude factor) producing a third value near −29 nT" lumps two distinct internal formulas. Per WIN-025 globe-fingerprint, monitor.py produces −28.94 nT (BOU baseline × KAPPA) and a second formula using the latitude factor produces a different value. Needs clean separation of which multiplier produces which intermediate result.

SEC-8b.3 TLDR: "20th-century" label inaccurate. Kim & Chang (2018) spans historical and 20th/21st-century data. Recommend: "historical eclipse-magnetic" or "20th/21st-century" to avoid the date-span error.

SEC-8b.3: "also predict" is loose wording. "Calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits... predict the same −17 to −21 nT range" + "also" suggests independent agreement; the actual claim is structural (same baseline × coverage × weighting arithmetic yields overlapping range, not independent confirmation). Recommend: "are consistent with" or "produce overlapping predictions for" instead of "also predict."

WIN-012.c5 H1 (critical, from curmudgeon c5 review): Fabricated "E-PRED-C" prediction label still in current detail_evidence. The label "E-PRED-C" does not correspond to any real prediction in data/predictions.json. This is a phantom label introduced in an earlier expansion and never cleaned up. Must be replaced with either the correct prediction ID or descriptive text. Noted as out-of-scope for EXP-201 but unresolved and highest-severity c5 finding.
